data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** [collegescorecard.ed.gov/data](https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/) | Acquisition side-loop: downloaded → catalog `downloaded`; raw=`data/raw/college-scorecard/`; files=[openalex-search.pdf, openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, +1 more]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Pooled institution-level earnings mask major-composition reversal: an institution with high average earnings may rank below another within STEM majors.

data.Title: Hoxby & Avery (2013)
data.Citation: Hoxby & Avery (2013). "The Missing 'One-Offs': The Hidden Supply of High-Achieving, Low-Income Students.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** College Board / ACT data (restricted); summary data in paper | Acquisition side-loop: downloaded → catalog `downloaded`; raw=`data/raw/hoxby-avery-2013/`; files=[openalex-search.pdf, openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** High-achieving low-income students nationally apply to less selective schools than high-income students with same scores — geographic isolation confounds pooled achievement-selectivity correlation.

data.Title: Espenshade & Radford (2009)
data.Citation: Espenshade & Radford (2009)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NSCE survey (proprietary); summary statistics published | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/espenshade-radford-2009/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Within legacies and athletes, racial gaps narrow substantially.

data.Title: Bound, Lovenheim & Turner (2010)
data.Citation: Bound, Lovenheim & Turner (2010). "Why Have College Completion Rates Declined?."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NLS/NELS/HSB — [nces.ed.gov](https://nces.ed.gov/) | Acquisition side-loop: portal_manual → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/bound-lovenheim-turner-2010/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Overall graduation rates stable; stratifying by institution type shows large declines at less-selective schools masked by stable elite rates.

data.Title: Carnevale & Strohl (2010)
data.Citation: Carnevale & Strohl (2010). "How Increasing College Access Is Increasing Inequality.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NCES IPEDS + BPS — [nces.ed.gov/surveys/bps](https://nces.ed.gov/surveys/bps/) | Acquisition side-loop: portal_manual → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/carnevale-strohl-2010/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Top institutions become more diverse over time in aggregate; stratifying by socioeconomic tier shows increasing stratification within selectivity.

data.Title: Hoxby & Murarka (2009)
data.Citation: Hoxby & Murarka (2009). "Charter Schools in New York City: Who Enrolls and How They Affect Their Students' Achievement.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NYC DOE administrative data; lottery data in paper | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/hoxby-murarka-2009/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Aggregate charter effect is small; stratifying by neighborhood poverty, lottery-admitted students show large gains — selection confounds pooled estimate.

data.Title: Rothstein (2004)
data.Citation: Rothstein (2004). "College Performance Predictions and the SAT.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** College Board restricted data (summary available) | PDF side-loop: verified manuscript PDF; 1 table(s) in `data/raw/rothstein-2004/table-extract.json`; manuscript=http://cle.berkeley.edu/wp/wp45.pdf
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Pooled SAT-GPA correlation is strong; within-institution, the correlation weakens or reverses for under-represented groups due to range restriction.

data.Title: ACCORD Study Group (2008)
data.Citation: ACCORD Study Group (2008). "Effects of Intensive Glucose Lowering in Type 2 Diabetes.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NHLBI BioLINCC — [biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov](https://biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/accord/) | Acquisition side-loop: portal_manual → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/accord-study-group-2008/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Intensive arm shows increased mortality overall; subgroup by baseline HbA1c may reverse benefit — severity confounds pooled result.

data.Title: ALLHAT Collaborative Research Group (2002)
data.Citation: ALLHAT Collaborative Research Group (2002). "Major Outcomes in High-Risk Hypertensive Patients.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NHLBI BioLINCC — [biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/allhat](https://biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/allhat/) | Acquisition side-loop: portal_manual → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/allhat-collaborative-research-group-2002/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Chlorthalidone beats lisinopril overall; within Black patients with hypertension, the margin reverses for certain outcomes.

data.Title: ISIS-2 Collaborative Group (1988)
data.Citation: ISIS-2 Collaborative Group (1988). "Randomised Trial of Intravenous Streptokinase, Oral Aspirin, Both, or Neither among 17,187 Cases of Suspected Acute Myocardial Infarction.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Summary tables in paper; data access via CTSU Oxford | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/isis-2-collaborative-group-1988/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Aspirin benefit pooled is robust; stratified by astrological sign (the paper's famous negative control), apparent reversals demonstrate the paradox mechanism.

data.Title: PLATO Trial (Wallentin et al. 2009)
data.Citation: PLATO Trial (Wallentin et al. 2009). "Ticagrelor versus Clopidogrel in Patients with Acute Coronary Syndromes.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** AstraZeneca data sharing — [clinicaltrialsdata.com](https://www.clinicaltrialsdata.com/) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/plato-trial/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Ticagrelor benefits globally; North America subgroup shows reversal — aspirin dose confounded the regional stratum.

data.Title: Fibrinolytic Therapy Trialists (1994)
data.Citation: Fibrinolytic Therapy Trialists (1994). "Indications for Fibrinolytic Therapy in Suspected Acute Myocardial Infarction.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Collaborative meta-analysis data; tables in paper | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/fibrinolytic-therapy-trialists-1994/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Benefit is large when treated early; late-treatment stratum shows no benefit or harm — pooled average masks time-dependent reversal.

data.Title: CAST (1989)
data.Citation: CAST (1989). "Preliminary Report: Effect of Encainide and Flecainide on Mortality.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Summary available; full data via NHLBI | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/cast-1989/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Drug suppresses arrhythmias in all strata; mortality increases in all strata — a cautionary reversal of surrogate endpoints.

data.Title: Women's Health Initiative (2002)
data.Citation: Women's Health Initiative (2002). "Risks and Benefits of Estrogen Plus Progestin in Healthy Postmenopausal Women.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NHLBI BioLINCC — [biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/whi](https://biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/whi/) | Acquisition side-loop: portal_manual → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/women-s-health-initiative-2002/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** WHI shows increased risk overall; stratifying by age at start, women who began HRT near menopause show cardioprotective benefit — "timing hypothesis."

data.Title: DCCT Research Group (1993)
data.Citation: DCCT Research Group (1993). "The Effect of Intensive Treatment of Diabetes on the Development and Progression of Long-Term Complications.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NIDDK data repository — [repository.niddk.nih.gov](https://repository.niddk.nih.gov/studies/dcct/) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/dcct-research-group-1993/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Overall benefit of intensive therapy; hypoglycemia risk reverses benefit in high-risk baseline strata.

data.Title: TRITON-TIMI 38 (Wiviott et al. 2007)
data.Citation: TRITON-TIMI 38 (Wiviott et al. 2007). "Prasugrel versus Clopidogrel in Patients with Acute Coronary Syndromes.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Eli Lilly data sharing — [vivli.org](https://vivli.org/) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/triton-timi-38/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Prasugrel superior overall; within prior-stroke stratum, net clinical outcome reverses due to bleeding risk.

data.Title: Yusuf et al. / HOPE Trial (2000)
data.Citation: Yusuf et al. / HOPE Trial (2000). "Effects of an Angiotensin-Converting-Enzyme Inhibitor, Ramipril, on Cardiovascular Events in High-Risk Patients.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NHLBI BioLINCC — [biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/hope](https://biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/hope/) | Acquisition side-loop: portal_manual → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/yusuf-et-al-hope-trial-2000/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Pooled benefit is clear; within-diabetic stratum the effect size is larger, masking a potential reversal in lower-risk subgroups.

data.Title: SPRINT Research Group (2015)
data.Citation: SPRINT Research Group (2015). "A Randomized Trial of Intensive versus Standard Blood-Pressure Control.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NHLBI BioLINCC — [biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/sprint](https://biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/sprint/) | Acquisition side-loop: portal_manual → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/sprint-research-group-2015/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Overall intensive benefit; within advanced CKD, benefit reverses — kidney function is the confounding stratum.

data.Title: AstraZeneca MYSTIC Trial / PACIFIC Trial crossover (2017–2019)
data.Citation: AstraZeneca MYSTIC Trial / PACIFIC Trial crossover (2017–2019)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Clinicaltrials.gov NCT02453282 / NCT02125461; AZ data sharing | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/astrazeneca-mystic-trial-pacific-trial-crossover-2017/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Pooled OS benefit is present; within low-PD-L1 stratum, benefit reverses — expression confounds pooled result.

data.Title: UK Biobank / UKBB Observational (ongoing) statins and cancer.
data.Citation: UK Biobank / UKBB Observational (ongoing) statins and cancer.
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** UK Biobank — [ukbiobank.ac.uk](https://www.ukbiobank.ac.uk/) | Acquisition side-loop: downloaded → catalog `downloaded`; raw=`data/raw/uk-biobank-ukbb-observational/`; files=[openalex-search.pdf, openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Pooled statin-cancer association appears protective; stratifying by indication (healthy user bias), benefit reduces or reverses in low-risk groups.

data.Title: Hiester (1984)
data.Citation: Hiester (1984). "Simpson's Paradox: Stats Often Can Deceive.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-71. ** [sabr.org/journal/article/simpsons-paradox-stats-often-can-deceive](https://sabr.org/journal/article/simpsons-paradox-stats-often-can-deceive/) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/hiester-1984/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Dawson vs. Lacy, Garcia vs. Griffey — multiple reversal cases identified. Sample size allocation across strata is the confounder.

data.Title: Efron & Morris (1975)
data.Citation: Efron & Morris (1975). "Data Analysis Using Stein's Estimator and Its Generalizations.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-71. ** 1970 MLB season; replicated data in paper | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/efron-morris-1975/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Empirical Bayes shrinkage reverses naive rankings at individual-player level within strata.

data.Title: Soccer Pass Completion by League (2017–2023) StatsBomb open data
data.Citation: Soccer Pass Completion by League (2017–2023) StatsBomb open data
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** [statsbomb.com/data](https://statsbomb.com/data/) (free open data) | Acquisition side-loop: downloaded → catalog `downloaded`; raw=`data/raw/soccer-pass-completion-by-league-2017/`; files=[openalex-search.pdf, openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, +1 more]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Teams in high-pressing leagues appear to have lower pass completion; stratifying by defensive pressure, passing accuracy reverses across leagues.

data.Title: Marathon Finishing Times by Age and Temperature Boston Marathon (2000–2019)
data.Citation: Marathon Finishing Times by Age and Temperature Boston Marathon (2000–2019)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** BAA public results — [baa.org/races/boston-marathon/results](https://www.baa.org/races/boston-marathon/results) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/marathon-finishing-times-by-age-and-temperature-boston-marat/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Older runners appear faster in hot years (they drop out less); stratifying by completion rate, the age-performance gradient reverses.

data.Title: Tennis First Serve Percentage by Surface (2010–2022)
data.Citation: Tennis First Serve Percentage by Surface (2010–2022)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** ATP/WTA open stats — [atptour.com/en/stats](https://www.atptour.com/en/stats); [wtatennis.com/stats](https://www.wtatennis.com/stats) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/tennis-first-serve-percentage-by-surface-2010/`; files=[openalex-search.html, openalex-metadata.json, source-2.html, acquisition.json, +1 more]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Player with higher overall first-serve win % may lose on every surface individually — surface allocation confounds overall rate.

data.Title: NFL Quarterback Rating by Game Situation (2010–2022)
data.Citation: NFL Quarterback Rating by Game Situation (2010–2022)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NFL.com / Pro-Football-Reference — [pro-football-reference.com](https://www.pro-football-reference.com/) | Acquisition side-loop: metadata_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/nfl-quarterback-rating-by-game-situation-2010/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** QB forced to throw more in losing situations (garbage time) inflates or deflates overall rate; within game-script strata, rankings reverse.

data.Title: Ellsbury vs. Lowell (2007–2008) cited in sports Simpson's Paradox literature
data.Citation: Ellsbury vs. Lowell (2007–2008) cited in sports Simpson's Paradox literature
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-71. ** Baseball-Reference.com (public) | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/ellsbury-vs-lowell-2007/`; files=[acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Ellsbury outperformed Lowell each individual season but had far fewer at-bats in 2007, reversing the combined average.

data.Title: NHL Shooting Percentage by Game State (2010–2022)
data.Citation: NHL Shooting Percentage by Game State (2010–2022)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Natural Stat Trick — [naturalstattrick.com](https://www.naturalstattrick.com/) (public) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/nhl-shooting-percentage-by-game-state-2010/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Players who play more "desperation minutes" while trailing inflate shot counts at lower %, reversing overall rates vs. within-score-state rates.

data.Title: Soccer Expected Goals (xG) by Home/Away and Opponent Tier
data.Citation: Soccer Expected Goals (xG) by Home/Away and Opponent Tier
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** FBRef — [fbref.com](https://fbref.com/en/) (public) | PDF side-loop: verified manuscript PDF; 1 table(s) in `data/raw/soccer-expected-goals/table-extract.json`; manuscript=https://pure.eur.nl/ws/files/48173682/Repub_115732.pdf
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Teams playing many home games against weak opponents inflate overall xG; within home-vs-tier stratum, ranking reverses for some clubs.

data.Title: Wade Boggs vs. Don Mattingly (multiple seasons) classic baseball paradox pair
data.Citation: Wade Boggs vs. Don Mattingly (multiple seasons) classic baseball paradox pair
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-68. ** Baseball-Reference.com (public) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/wade-boggs-vs-don-mattingly/`; files=[openalex-search.html, openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** One player may outperform the other in every individual season but have lower career average due to at-bat weighting across seasons.

data.Title: Lollar vs. McCatty Pitching (1982–1983) from Hiester (1984) SABR
data.Citation: Lollar vs. McCatty Pitching (1982–1983) from Hiester (1984) SABR
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Baseball-Reference.com (public) | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/lollar-vs-mccatty-pitching-1982/`; files=[acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** McCatty had higher win % in both years individually; Lollar's combined two-year win % exceeded McCatty's.

data.Title: MLB Park Factor Adjustments (Statcast era, 2015–2023)
data.Citation: MLB Park Factor Adjustments (Statcast era, 2015–2023)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Baseball Savant — [baseballsavant.mlb.com](https://baseballsavant.mlb.com/) (public) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/mlb-park-factor-adjustments/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Player in extreme homer-friendly park may appear a better home-run hitter overall; stratifying by park, ranking reverses.

data.Title: Olympic Sprint Times by Heat Type (1996–2020)
data.Citation: Olympic Sprint Times by Heat Type (1996–2020)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** World Athletics database — [worldathletics.org](https://worldathletics.org/records/by-discipline/sprints/) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/olympic-sprint-times-by-heat-type-1996/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Athletes peaking early may have faster preliminary times; stratifying by heat round and wind, rankings reverse for finals-only comparison.

data.Title: von Kügelgen, Mohamed & Schölkopf (2021)
data.Citation: von Kügelgen, Mohamed & Schölkopf (2021). "Simpson's Paradox in COVID-19 Case Fatality Rates.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** WHO/CDC age-stratified CFR — [who.int/data](https://www.who.int/data) | Acquisition side-loop: portal_manual → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/von-kugelgen-mohamed-scholkopf-2021/`; files=[openalex-search.html, openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, +1 more]
data.ReversalMechanism: See expansion plan.

data.Title: Dagan et al. (2021)
data.Citation: Dagan et al. (2021). "BNT162b2 mRNA Covid-19 Vaccine in a Nationwide Mass Vaccination Setting.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Clalit Health Services administrative data; methodology replicated with Israeli MoH open tables | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/dagan-et-al-2021/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Apparent VE may flip at population level during booster rollout when younger unvaccinated cohorts have lower baseline risk.

data.Title: Israeli Ministry of Health (Aug–Sep 2021)
data.Citation: Israeli Ministry of Health (Aug–Sep 2021). "Vaccine Effectiveness."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Israeli MoH open reports — [gov.il/en/departments/ministry_of_health](https://www.gov.il/en/departments/ministry_of_health) | Acquisition side-loop: downloaded → catalog `downloaded`; raw=`data/raw/israeli-ministry-of-health/`; files=[openalex-search.pdf, openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Naive data showed more vaccinated than unvaccinated in hospitals; stratifying by age, VE is strongly positive in all groups — age confounds the pooled comparison.

data.Title: Yule (1903)
data.Citation: Yule (1903). "Notes on the Theory of Association of Attributes in Statistics.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Late 19th century registry data; reproduced in statistics textbooks | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/yule-1903/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Inoculated appeared to have higher death rates pooled; age was the suppressed confounder.

data.Title: Mantel & Haenszel (1959)
data.Citation: Mantel & Haenszel (1959). "Statistical Aspects of the Analysis of Data from Retrospective Studies of Disease.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Methodological paper; data from Doll & Hill series | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/mantel-haenszel-1959/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Methodology paper demonstrating how stratification resolves apparent reversals in pooled odds ratios.

data.Title: Doll & Hill (1954)
data.Citation: Doll & Hill (1954). "The Mortality of Doctors in Relation to Their Smoking Habits.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** British Doctors Study — [ctsu.ox.ac.uk](https://www.ctsu.ox.ac.uk/research/uk-doctors-cohort) | Acquisition side-loop: metadata_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/doll-hill-1954/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Age confounds naive cross-sectional comparison; longitudinal follow-up resolves apparent reversals.

data.Title: Hammond & Horn (1958)
data.Citation: Hammond & Horn (1958). "Smoking and Death Rates.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** ACS Cancer Prevention Study; summary in paper | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/hammond-horn-1958/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Pooled mortality ratios are high; stratifying by prior illness status reveals healthy smoker bias.

data.Title: Polack et al. (2020)
data.Citation: Polack et al. (2020). "Safety and Efficacy of the BNT162b2 mRNA Covid-19 Vaccine.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Pfizer data sharing — [pfizer.com/research/clinical-trials](https://www.pfizer.com/research/clinical-trials/pfizer-clinical-trial-data-sharing) | Acquisition side-loop: metadata_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/polack-et-al-2020/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Overall VE 95%; within elderly subgroup with comorbidities, confidence intervals widen — small subgroup VE point estimates vary.

data.Title: Baden et al. (2021)
data.Citation: Baden et al. (2021). "Efficacy and Safety of the mRNA-1273 SARS-CoV-2 Vaccine.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Moderna data sharing — [vivli.org](https://vivli.org/) | Acquisition side-loop: downloaded → catalog `downloaded`; raw=`data/raw/baden-et-al-2021/`; files=[openalex-search.pdf, openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, +1 more]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Within-subgroup VE estimates vary; minority subgroups under-enrolled, creating allocation-distortion risk in any stratified comparison.

data.Title: Flannery et al. (2020)
data.Citation: Flannery et al. (2020). "Influenza Vaccine Effectiveness against Pediatric Deaths.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** CDC FluVEIN — [cdc.gov/flu/vaccines-work/vaccine-effectiveness.htm](https://www.cdc.gov/flu/vaccines-work/vaccine-effectiveness.htm) | PDF side-loop: verified manuscript PDF; 1 table(s) in `data/raw/flannery-et-al-2020/table-extract.json`; manuscript=https://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/volumes/69/rr/pdfs/rr6908a1-H.pdf
data.ReversalMechanism: ** VE appears lower in high-mismatch seasons; stratifying by health status of child, VE in healthy children is higher than pooled across risk groups.

data.Title: Simonsen et al. (2005)
data.Citation: Simonsen et al. (2005). "Influenza Vaccination and Mortality Benefit: New Insights, New Opportunities.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Medicare claims data; summary in paper | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/simonsen-et-al-2005/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Healthy vaccinee bias — vaccinated elderly appear to have 50% lower all-cause mortality; stratifying by baseline functional status reduces effect to near zero.

data.Title: Hernan, Hernandez-Diaz & Robins (2004)
data.Citation: Hernan, Hernandez-Diaz & Robins (2004). "A Structural Approach to Selection Bias.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Nurses' Health Study — [nurseshealthstudy.org](https://www.nurseshealthstudy.org/) | Acquisition side-loop: metadata_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/hernan-hernandez-diaz-robins-2004/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Early observational studies showed HRT protective; stratifying by when HRT was initiated reveals the timing-hypothesis reversal (harmful if started late).

data.Title: Cornfield et al. (1959)
data.Citation: Cornfield et al. (1959). "Smoking and Lung Cancer: Recent Evidence and a Discussion of Some Questions.."

data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-68. ** Linked birth/death records — [cdc.gov/nchs/data_access/vitalstatsonline.htm](https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data_access/vitalstatsonline.htm) | Acquisition side-loop: downloaded → catalog `downloaded`; raw=`data/raw/hernandez-diaz-schisterman-hernan-2006/`; files=[openalex-search.pdf, openalex-metadata.json, source-1.html, acquisition.json, +1 more]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Low birth-weight infants of smokers have paradoxically lower mortality than low birth-weight infants of non-smokers — classic collider/selection-bias reversal (birth weight is a collider).

data.ReversalMechanism: ** Female faculty also rate female applicants lower — gender of evaluator moderates but does not eliminate the reversal.

data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Single large organization HR records; methodology in paper | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/castilla-2008/`; files=[openalex-metadata.json, acquisition.json, provenance.md]

data.ReversalMechanism: ** Women promoted at lower rates overall; controlling for publications and field, gap persists — field of specialty is a partial confounder.

data.Citation: HMDA (Home Mortgage Disclosure Act) Data (annual, 1990–present)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** [ffiec.cfpb.gov/data-browser](https://ffiec.cfpb.gov/data-browser/) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/hmda/`; files=[source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Raw denial rate gap Black/White is ~2×; stratifying by loan-to-income and property type, gap shrinks significantly but does not reverse — Type C compression.

data.Title: Munnell, Tootell, Browne & McEneaney (1996)
data.Citation: Munnell, Tootell, Browne & McEneaney (1996). "Mortgage Lending in Boston: Interpreting HMDA Data.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** Federal Reserve Bank of Boston survey data; methodology published | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/munnell-tootell-browne-mceneaney-1996/`; files=[acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Controlling for financial characteristics reduces but does not eliminate racial gap — unobserved creditworthiness is the partial confounder.

data.Title: Bartlett, Morse, Stanton & Wallace (2022)
data.Citation: Bartlett, Morse, Stanton & Wallace (2022). "Consumer-Lending Discrimination in the FinTech Era.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** HMDA + GSE loan-level data — [fanniemae.com/research-and-insights](https://www.fanniemae.com/research-and-insights) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/bartlett-morse-stanton-wallace-2022/`; files=[source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** FinTech lenders show smaller racial pricing gaps than face-to-face; stratifying by credit score tier, the pattern reverses for some score ranges.

data.Title: Federal Reserve SCF (Survey of Consumer Finances, triennial)
data.Citation: Federal Reserve SCF (Survey of Consumer Finances, triennial)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** [federalreserve.gov/econres/scfindex.htm](https://www.federalreserve.gov/econres/scfindex.htm) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/federal-reserve-scf/`; files=[source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Racial wealth gap is enormous; stratifying by income quartile, within upper-income brackets the gap shrinks dramatically but the composition effect confounds cross-group comparisons.

data.Title: CFPB Consumer Credit Panel (CCP) — New York Fed
data.Citation: CFPB Consumer Credit Panel (CCP) — New York Fed
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** [newyorkfed.org/microeconomics/hhdc](https://www.newyorkfed.org/microeconomics/hhdc) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/cfpb-consumer-credit-panel/`; files=[source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Regional delinquency rates appear uniform; stratifying by zip-code income tier reveals dramatic reversal — low-income zips in high-income states look similar to high-income zips in low-income states.

data.Title: SBA Small Business Lending Data (annual)
data.Citation: SBA Small Business Lending Data (annual)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** [sba.gov/about-sba/sba-data-and-research](https://www.sba.gov/about-sba/sba-data-and-research) | Acquisition side-loop: blocked → catalog `blocked`; raw=`data/raw/sba-small-business-lending-data/`; files=[acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Minority-owned businesses receive smaller loans on average; stratifying by business revenue and industry, approval rates reverse in some sectors.

data.Title: Petersen & Rajan (1994)
data.Citation: Petersen & Rajan (1994). "The Benefits of Lending Relationships: Evidence from Small Business Data.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NSSBF — [federalreserve.gov/releases/nssbf](https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/nssbf/) | Acquisition side-loop: blocked → catalog `blocked`; raw=`data/raw/petersen-rajan-1994/`; files=[acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Older relationships get more credit; young firms with long relationships get more than old firms with short relationships — relationship length confounds firm-age effect.

data.Title: Berger & Udell (1995)
data.Citation: Berger & Udell (1995). "Relationship Lending and Lines of Credit in Small Firm Finance.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** NSSBF (see above) | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/berger-udell-1995/`; files=[acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Long-relationship borrowers get lower rates; controlling for firm quality, the relationship effect reverses for very small firms.

data.Title: FDIC Small Business Lending Survey (2016)
data.Citation: FDIC Small Business Lending Survey (2016)
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** [fdic.gov/bank/statistical/guide](https://www.fdic.gov/bank/statistical/guide/) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/fdic-small-business-lending-survey-2016/`; files=[source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Community banks appear to approve small business loans more readily; stratifying by loan size, large banks approve more small-balance loans.

data.Title: Keys, Mukherjee, Seru & Vig (2010)
data.Citation: Keys, Mukherjee, Seru & Vig (2010). "Did Securitization Lead to Lax Screening? Evidence from Subprime Loans.."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** LPS Applied Analytics (now Black Knight); methodology in paper | Acquisition side-loop: manual_only → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/keys-mukherjee-seru-vig-2010/`; files=[acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Securitized loans default at higher rates overall; within narrow FICO bands at the threshold, the effect reverses due to screening discontinuity.

data.Title: Acharya, Imbierowicz, Steffen & Teichmann (2020)
data.Citation: Acharya, Imbierowicz, Steffen & Teichmann (2020). "Does the Lack of Financial Stability Impair the Transmission of Monetary Policy?."
data.DataSourceNote: Imported loop-77. ** BLS/FRED bank-level data — [fred.stlouisfed.org](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/) | Acquisition side-loop: landing_saved → catalog `manual_only`; raw=`data/raw/acharya-imbierowicz-steffen-teichmann-2020/`; files=[source-1.html, acquisition.json, provenance.md]
data.ReversalMechanism: ** Monetary easing appears to increase lending overall; within low-capitalization banks, lending contracts rather than expands — capitalization is the confounding stratum.

data.RowDescription: One StratumVariables row naming the confounder before classification.
Two Treatments rows (A and B) with human-readable descriptions.
One Strata row per stratum with StratumLabel and Description.
2×K CaseCells: every (stratum × treatment) with Cases and Successes.
data.RequiredFields: CaseCellId, Study, StratumLabel, TreatmentLabel, Cases, Successes

data.RowDescription: Mark catalog row imported: IngestionStatus=imported, LinkedStudyId set.
One StratumSummaries row per (stratum × treatment) — keys for derived rates.
One TreatmentRankings row (A vs B) with AdjustmentRationale.
data.MechanicalCheck: Exactly one ranking row per study.

schema.name: NFixedB

schema.Description: Allocation-free gap: sum_s (n_s/N)*(rate_As-rate_Bs). Invariant under within-stratum reallocation.

DiscoveryHypotheses.Description: Table: DiscoveryHypotheses — pre-registered empirical claims tested against the corpus at loop-61. Each row states an expected pattern before querying the DAG.

schema.name: HypothesisId
schema.Description: Unique slug (e.g. H-latent-d).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: Statement
schema.Description: Natural-language claim under test.

schema.name: ExpectedOutcome
schema.Description: Pass criterion in plain language.

schema.name: RegisteredInLoop
schema.Description: LoopId where this hypothesis was registered.

schema.name: TraditionId
schema.Description: FK → ResearchTraditions.TraditionId.

schema.name: EpistemicTier
schema.Description: consistency-check (definition-linked regression) | corpus-hypothesis (contingent corpus pattern)

data.Statement: More than half of Type-D (SAFE) studies would flip their pooled winner under counterfactual allocation reweighting.
Every sign-flip study has SignalPurity strictly below 0.5.
Allocation-stable Type-D studies carry larger observed pooled gaps than latent Type-D studies.
Confounder-labeled studies produce manifest sign-flips at observed allocation (IsSignFlip=TRUE) far more often than collider/selection studies — reversal mode differs by mechanism class.
Collider/selection studies produce latent-only flips under allocation sweep without manifest sign-flips at observed allocation.
IsParadoxExplained is TRUE for all and only confounder sign-flip studies — adjust-resolvable confounding is the unique condition that makes a reversal fully explained.
Studies with IsSignFlip=TRUE and CausalRole != confounder (contested or mediator) have IsParadoxExplained=FALSE.
Catalog ExpectedDistortionType exact-match rate is below 50% — curator tags behave as mechanism priors, not taxonomy predictors.
Collider and selection studies produce zero manifest sign-flips (DistortionType A or B) at observed allocation — including hernandez-diaz birth-weight collider tagged Expected A in catalog.
C+ studies carry higher mean AllocationDistortion than C- studies and Type-D studies — magnitude inflation is the largest quantitative error class and invisible to is_sign_flip.

At least four Type-D studies are sweep-fragile: signal_purity=1, allocation_distortion=0, sweep_pooled_gap_range>0.3, allocation_fragility>=10 — perfectly safe by pooled metrics yet latent-flip under reweighting.
CorrectedGap is allocation-invariant for every study: MAX(SweepStudySummary.SweepCorrectedGapRange) < 0.0001 and zero failing studies.
IsParadoxExplained ⟺ confounder manifest sign-flip: ExplainedConfounderCount = ConfounderSignFlipCount, no false-positive explained rows, no unexplained confounder sign-flips.
data.ExpectedOutcome: ExplainedConfounderCount = ConfounderSignFlipCount AND FalsePositiveExplainedCount = 0 AND UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount = 0

data.Statement: At scale (≥10 collider/selection studies post expansion), zero manifest sign-flips: ColliderSelectionManifestCount = 0.
Four Category=theorem conclusions are witnessed: SignalPurity (conc-12/20) plus three new theorems from loops 73–75.

DiscoveryFindings.Description: Table: DiscoveryFindings — witnessed outcomes for each pre-registered DiscoveryHypothesis. IsConfirmed is computed live from ModelSummary aggregates.

schema.name: FindingId
schema.Description: Unique slug; one row per hypothesis.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: HypothesisId
schema.Description: FK → DiscoveryHypotheses.HypothesisId.

schema.name: HypothesisStatement
schema.Description: Lookup: DiscoveryHypotheses.Statement.

schema.name: ObservedMetric
schema.Description: Witnessed metric value from ModelSummary for this hypothesis.
schema.formula: =IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-latent-d", CONCAT("fraction=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-purity", CONCAT("maxPurity=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-small-effect", CONCAT("stable=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]), " latent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-econ-zero", CONCAT("flips=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-dist", CONCAT("epi=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyAvgDistortion]), " edu=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationAvgDistortion])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-causal-manifest", CONCAT("confFlip=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), " collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-causal-latent", CONCAT("collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]), " collLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount]), " collN=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-explained-confounder", CONCAT("ExplainedConfounderCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]), ", ConfounderSignFlipCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-unexplained-nonconfounder", CONCAT("ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-catalog-exact-match", CONCAT("exactRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-catalog-flip-prediction", CONCAT("flipPredRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", CONCAT("econHighImbFlips=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount]), "; epiRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]), "; legal=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LegalHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]), "; sports=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsHighImbalanceSignFlipRate])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-no-manifest-v2", CONCAT("collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-cplus-magnitude", CONCAT("C+=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CPlusAvgDistortion]), " C-=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CMinusAvgDistortion]), " D=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypeDAvgDistortion])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-econ-encoding-selection", CONCAT("EconExpectedAMismatchRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsExpectedAMismatchRate])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-profiles-stable", CONCAT("eduLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationLatentFraction]), " sportsLatent=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SportsLatentFraction]), " econFlipRate=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipRate])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-corrected-gap-invariant", CONCAT("maxRange=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[MaxStudySweepCorrectedGapRange]), " fails=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[CorrectedGapInvariantFailCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-explained-bidirectional", CONCAT("explained=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]), " confFlip=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), " falsePos=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[FalsePositiveExplainedCount]), " unexplained=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[UnexplainedConfounderSignFlipCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-no-manifest-theorem", CONCAT("collN=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionCount]), " collManifest=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount])), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-theorem-portfolio", CONCAT("theoremCount=", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TheoremCount])), ""))))))))))))))))))))

schema.name: IsConfirmed
schema.Description: TRUE when the witnessed metric satisfies the pre-registered ExpectedOutcome.
schema.formula: =IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-latent-d", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[LatentTypeDFraction]) > 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-purity", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipSignalPurityMax]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-small-effect", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapStableD]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[AvgPooledGapLatentD]), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-econ-zero", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsSignFlipCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-dist", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyAvgDistortion]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EducationAvgDistortion]), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-causal-manifest", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) > LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-causal-latent", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionLatentOnlyCount]) >= 5), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-explained-confounder", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) = LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ConfounderSignFlipCount]), LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ExplainedConfounderCount]) >= 10), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-unexplained-nonconfounder", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ContestedOrMediatorExplainedCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-catalog-exact-match", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[TypePredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-catalog-flip-prediction", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[SignFlipPredictionMatchRate]) < 0.5, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-domain-flip-geometry-controlled", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EconomicsHighImbalanceSignFlipCount]) = 0, LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[EpidemiologyHighImbalanceSignFlipRate]) > 0.15), IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-collider-no-manifest-v2", LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], ModelSummary[ColliderSelectionManifestCount]) = 0, IF({{HypothesisId}} = "H-cplus-magnitude", AND(LOOKUP("simpsons-paradox-v1", ModelSummary[ModelSummaryId], data.Evidence: 18 sign-flip rows all have signal_purity < 0.5 (max=0.4827, jeter-justice). melanoma-altman-1991 is the known boundary case: C+ type, pooled_gap_crosses_zero=TRUE, purity=0.479, but no sign flip. Zero counterexamples. Law holds unconditionally.

15/15 confounder sign-flips have IsParadoxExplained=TRUE; 0/78 C+/C-/D explained; birth-weight (mediator) and both berkeley rows (contested) are FALSE. Invariant inv-explained-sign-flip-confounder PASS 15/0.
berkeley-1973, berkeley-six-dept-1973 (contested), birth-weight-paradox (mediator) all have IsParadoxExplained=FALSE. Zero exceptions among non-confounder sign-flips.

data.ObservedMetric: exactRate=0.194 (18/93); biggest bucket Expected A→D: 23 studies
